1.anchor: I am writing to all of God’s holy people in Philippi who belong to Christ Jesus, including the church leaders

11.anchor: May you always be filled with the fruit of your salvation—the righteous character produced in your life by Jesus Christ
11.note: Greek with the fruit of righteousness through Jesus Christ.

12.anchor: And I want you to know, my dear brothers and sisters,

13.anchor: For everyone here, including the whole palace guard,

14.anchor: And because of my imprisonment, most of the believers
here have gained confidence and boldly speak God’s message
14.note: Some manuscripts read speak the message.

6.note: Or Being in the form of God.

7.anchor: Instead, he gave up his divine privileges
he took the humble position of a slave
7.note: Or the form of a slave.

7.anchor: When he appeared in human form,
7.note: Some English translations put this phrase in verse 8.

17.anchor: But I will rejoice even if I lose my life, pouring it out like a liquid offering to God,
17.note: Greek I will rejoice even if I am to be poured out as a liquid offering.

29.anchor: Welcome him in the Lord’s love

1.anchor: Therefore, my dear brothers and sisters,

3.anchor: And I ask you, my true partner,

5.anchor: Let everyone see that you are considerate in all you do. Remember, the Lord is coming soon.

13.anchor: For I can do everything through Christ,

23.anchor: May the grace of the Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit.

1.anchor: Whatever happens, my dear brothers and sisters,

3.anchor: For we who worship by the Spirit of God
3.note: Some manuscripts read worship God in spirit; worship in spirit.

9.anchor: and become one with him. I no longer count on my own righteousness through obeying the law; rather, I become righteous through faith in Christ.
9.note: Or through the faithfulness of Christ.

13.anchor: No, dear brothers and sisters, I have not achieved it,
13.note: Some manuscripts read not yet achieved it.
